annotations.label: Atiyah–Singer index theorem (statement)

anchor.snippet: states that for an elliptic differential operator on a compact manifold

annotations.note: No notion of elliptic differential operator, analytical/topological index, or the index theorem exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: The manifold X

anchor.snippet: X is a compact smooth manifold

annotations.note: Smooth manifolds are formalized via `IsManifold`, with compactness expressed by the `CompactSpace` typeclass.

annotations.label: Vector bundles E and F

anchor.snippet: E and F are smooth vector bundles over X

annotations.note: Smooth vector bundles over a manifold are formalized as the `ContMDiffVectorBundle` mixin on a topological `VectorBundle`.

annotations.label: Elliptic differential operator D

anchor.snippet: D is an elliptic differential operator from E to F

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of differential operators between vector bundles or ellipticity thereof.

annotations.label: Symbol of a differential operator (Euclidean)

anchor.snippet: given by dropping all terms of order less than n and replacing

annotations.note: The principal symbol of a differential operator is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic operator (symbol nonzero)

anchor.snippet: The operator is called elliptic if the symbol is nonzero whenever at least one y is nonzero

annotations.note: No symbol-based notion of ellipticity exists in Mathlib (only elliptic curves/functions use the word).

annotations.label: Laplace and wave operator symbols

anchor.snippet: The Laplace operator in k variables has symbol

annotations.note: The symbol of the Laplace or wave operator is not formalized, as symbols are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symbol on a smooth manifold

anchor.snippet: The symbol of a differential operator of order n on a smooth manifold X is defined in much the same way

annotations.note: The symbol as a section over the cotangent bundle is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic operator on a manifold

anchor.snippet: The differential operator is called elliptic if the element of Hom

annotations.note: Ellipticity of an operator on a manifold via invertibility of its symbol is not formalized.

annotations.label: Existence of a parametrix

anchor.snippet: an elliptic operator D on a compact manifold has a (non-unique) parametrix

annotations.note: The notion and existence of a parametrix do not appear anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kernel is finite-dimensional

anchor.snippet: the kernel of D is finite-dimensional

annotations.note: The elliptic-regularity result that an elliptic operator has finite-dimensional kernel is not formalized.

annotations.label: Elliptic operator is Fredholm

anchor.snippet: As the elliptic differential operator D has a pseudoinverse, it is a Fredholm operator

annotations.note: Mathlib has only the Fredholm alternative for compact operators, not a Fredholm-operator class nor the elliptic-implies-Fredholm fact.

annotations.label: Analytical index

anchor.snippet: Any Fredholm operator has an index , defined as the difference between

annotations.note: The Fredholm index (dim ker − dim coker) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Index of d/dx − λ on the circle

anchor.snippet: Suppose that the manifold is the circle

annotations.note: This computed example of an analytical index is not present, since the index itself is unformalized.

annotations.label: Topological index

anchor.snippet: The topological index of an elliptic differential operator

annotations.note: The topological index (built from characteristic classes of the symbol) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simplified topological index via Euler class

anchor.snippet: In some situations, it is possible to simplify the above formula for computational purposes

annotations.note: Neither the Euler class nor Todd/Chern classes used in this simplification exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topological index via K-theory

anchor.snippet: One can also define the topological index using only K-theory

annotations.note: Topological K-theory of spaces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: The index problem

anchor.snippet: The index problem is the following: compute the (analytical) index of D using only the symbol

annotations.note: The index problem cannot be stated since neither the analytical index nor symbols are formalized.

annotations.label: Atiyah–Singer index theorem

anchor.snippet: The analytical index of D is equal to its topological index

annotations.note: The equality of analytical and topological index is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Integrality of the topological index

anchor.snippet: the Atiyah–Singer index theorem implies some deep integrality properties

annotations.note: These integrality consequences of the index theorem are not formalized.

annotations.label: Vanishing of the index

anchor.snippet: The index of an elliptic differential operator obviously vanishes if the operator is self adjoint

annotations.note: Vanishing of the index for self-adjoint/odd-dimensional cases is not formalized, as the index is undefined.

annotations.label: Index theorem / GRR commutative diagram

anchor.snippet: of compact stably almost complex manifolds, then there is a commutative diagram

annotations.note: Neither the index map nor the Grothendieck–Riemann–Roch comparison diagram exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Teleman index theorem

anchor.snippet: For any abstract elliptic operator

annotations.note: Teleman's index theorem for abstract elliptic operators is not formalized.

annotations.label: Connes–Donaldson–Sullivan–Teleman index theorem

anchor.snippet: For any quasiconformal manifold there exists a local construction of the Hirzebruch–Thom characteristic classes

annotations.note: Quasiconformal manifolds and this index theorem are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extension to pseudodifferential operators

anchor.snippet: The Atiyah–Singer theorem applies to elliptic pseudodifferential operators in much the same way as for elliptic differential operators

annotations.note: Pseudodifferential operators and the corresponding index theorem are not formalized.

annotations.label: Index theorem for elliptic complexes

anchor.snippet: it is sometimes more convenient to work with an elliptic complex

annotations.note: Elliptic complexes and their index are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: it is sometimes more convenient to work with an elliptic complex

annotations.label: Atiyah–Patodi–Singer (manifolds with boundary)

anchor.snippet: If the manifold is allowed to have boundary, then some restrictions must be put on the domain of the elliptic operator in order to ensure a finite index

annotations.note: The Atiyah–Patodi–Singer boundary index theorem (and eta invariant) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Family index theorem (K-theory of Y)

anchor.snippet: one can consider a family of elliptic operators parameterized by some space Y

annotations.note: The families index theorem valued in K-theory of the parameter space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Equivariant index theorem

anchor.snippet: If there is a group action of a group G on the compact manifold X , commuting with the elliptic operator

annotations.note: The equivariant index theorem with values in the representation ring is not formalized.

annotations.label: L² index theorem

anchor.snippet: Atiyah (1976) showed how to extend the index theorem to some non-compact manifolds

annotations.note: Atiyah's L² index theorem for covering spaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Callias index theorem

anchor.snippet: The Callias index theorem is an index theorem for a Dirac operator on a noncompact odd-dimensional space

annotations.note: The Callias index theorem and Dirac operators are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chern–Gauss–Bonnet theorem

anchor.snippet: The index formula for this operator yields the Chern–Gauss–Bonnet theorem

annotations.note: The Chern–Gauss–Bonnet theorem (and the Euler-class integrand) is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hirzebruch–Riemann–Roch theorem

anchor.snippet: Applying the index theorem, we obtain the Hirzebruch-Riemann-Roch theorem

annotations.note: No Riemann–Roch theorem (Todd/Chern character) is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hirzebruch signature theorem

anchor.snippet: The Hirzebruch signature theorem states that the signature of a compact oriented manifold X of dimension 4 k is given by the L genus of the manifold

annotations.note: The signature theorem and the L-genus are not formalized (only signatures of quadratic forms exist).

annotations.label: Integrality of the Â genus

anchor.snippet: Borel and Hirzebruch showed that it is integral for spin manifolds

annotations.note: The Â-genus and its integrality for spin manifolds are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rochlin's theorem

anchor.snippet: Rochlin's theorem that the signature of a 4-dimensional spin manifold is divisible by 16

annotations.note: Rochlin's divisibility theorem for spin 4-manifolds is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pseudodifferential operators

anchor.snippet: Pseudodifferential operators can be explained easily in the case of constant coefficient operators on Euclidean space

annotations.note: Pseudodifferential operators are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: McKean–Singer heat-equation index formula

anchor.snippet: Therefore, the index of D is given by

annotations.note: The McKean–Singer heat-kernel supertrace formula for the index is not formalized.
